data.description:
## Why
OutboundMutation.links is structurally present but always [] (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py ~993,1110); there is no fetcher extraction of issuelinks, no applier dispatch, and no inbound parsing. rebar ticket links (blocks/depends_on/relates_to/...) do NOT cross the bridge in either direction.

## What
- Outbound: extract rebar links and translate to Jira issue links (create/remove) in the applier.
- Inbound: parse Jira issuelinks in the fetcher snapshot and reflect into rebar relations.
- Bidirectional round-trip + echo-suppression (no re-import of links rebar itself wrote).

## Scope note
Surfaced by the task (c) coverage gap analysis (gap G4): a round-trip TEST is premature until the feature exists. Scoped OUT of task e143 (coverage) AC; this story implements the capability, after which the link round-trip test can be added.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Outbound link create/remove implemented + dispatched in the applier
- [ ] Inbound issuelinks parsed in the fetcher and reflected into rebar relations
- [ ] Bidirectional link round-trip converges with echo-suppression (no re-echo)
- [ ] Live DIG probe (issuelink create/read/delete) passes; suite green
data.title: Implement Jira reconciler link/relation sync (outbound+inbound)
